\\"url\\": \\"https://s3.amazonaws.com/models.huggingface.co/bert/pplm/discriminators/SST_classifier_head.pt\\",\\n        \\"class_size\\": 5,\\n        \\"embed_size\\": 1024,\\n        \\"class_vocab\\": {\\"very_positive\\": 2, \\"very_negative\\": 3},\\n        \\"default_class\\": 3,\\n        \\"pretrained_model\\": \\"gpt2-medium\\",\\n    },\\n    \\"toxicity\\": {\\n        \\"url\\": \\"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uber-research/PPLM/master/paper_code/discrim_models/toxicity_classifierhead.pt\\",\\n        \\"class_size\\": 2,\\n        \\"embed_size\\": 1024,\\n        \\"class_vocab\\": {\\"non_toxic\\": 0, \\"toxic\\": 1},\\n        \\"default_class\\": 0,\\n        \\"pretrained_model\\": \\"gpt2-medium\\",\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n\\ndef to_var(x, requires_grad=False, volatile=False, device=\\"cuda\\"):\\n    if torch.cuda.is_available() and device == \\"cuda\\":\\n        x = x.cuda()\\n    elif device != \\"cuda\\":\\n        x = x.to(device)\\n    return Variable(x, requires_grad=requires_grad, volatile=volatile)\\n\\n\\ndef top_k_filter(logits, k, probs=False):\\n    \\"\\"\\"\\n    Masks everything but the k top entries as -infinity (1e10).\\n    Used to mask logits such that e^-infinity -> 0 won\'t contribute to the\\n    sum of the denominator.\\n    \\"\\"\\"\\n    if k == 0:\\n        return logits\\n    else:\\n        values = torch.topk(logits, k)[0]\\n        batch_mins = values[:, -1].view(-1, 1).expand_as(logits)\\n        if probs:\\n            return torch.where(logits < batch_mins, torch.ones_like(logits) * 0.0, logits)\\n        return torch.where(logits < batch_mins, torch.ones_like(logits) * -BIG_CONST, logits)\\n\\n\\ndef get_classifier(name: str, device: str) -> ClassificationHead:\\n    params = DISCRIMINATOR_MODELS_PARAMS[name]\\n    classifier = ClassificationHead(class_size=params[\\"class_size\\"], embed_size=params[\\"embed_size\\"]).to(device)\\n    if \\"url\\" in params:\\n        resolved_archive_file = cached_path(params[\\"url\\"])\\n    elif \\"path\\" in params:\\n        resolved_archive_file = params[\\"path\\"]\\n    else:\\n        raise ValueError(\\"Either url or path have to be specified \\" \\"in the discriminator model parameters\\")\\n    classifier.load_state_dict(torch.load(resolved_archive_file, map_location=device))\\n    classifier.eval()\\n    return classifier\\n\\n\\ndef get_class_id(name: str, class_label: Union[str, int]) -> Optional[int]:\\n    params = DISCRIMINATOR_MODELS_PARAMS[name]\\n    if         print(\\"using default class {}\\".format(label_id))\\n    elif isinstance(class_label, int):\\n        if class_label in set(params[\\"class_vocab\\"].values()):\\n            label_id = class_label\\n        else:\\n            label_id = params[\\"default_class\\"]\\n            print(\\"class_label {} not in class_vocab\\".format(class_label))\\n            print(\\"available values are: {}\\".format(params[\\"class_vocab\\"]))\\n            print(\\"using default class {}\\".format(label_id))\\n    else:\\n        label_id = params[\\"default_class\\"]\\n    return label_id\\n\\n\\ndef perturb_past(\\n        past,\\n        model,\\n        last,\\n        unpert_past=None,\\n        unpert_logits=None,\\n        accumulated_hidden=None,\\n        grad_norms=None,\\n        stepsize=0.01,\\n        classifier=None,\\n        class_label=None,\\n        num_iterations=3,\\n        horizon_length=1,\\n        window_length=0,\\n        decay=False,\\n        gamma=1.5,\\n        kl_scale=0.01,\\n        device=\\"cuda\\",\\n):\\n    # Generate inital perturbed past\\n    grad_accumulator = [(np.zeros(p.shape).astype(\\"float32\\")) for p in past]\\n\\n    if accumulated_hidden is None:\\n        accumulated_hidden = 0\\n\\n    if decay:\\n        decay_mask = torch.arange(0.0, 1.0 + SMALL_CONST, 1.0 / (window_length))[1:]\\n    else:\\n        decay_mask = 1.0\\n\\n    # Generate a mask is gradient perturbated is based on a past window\\n    _, _, _, curr_length, _ = past[0].shape\\n\\n    if curr_length > window_length and window_length > 0:\\n        ones_key_val_shape = tuple(past[0].shape[:-2]) + tuple([window_length]) + tuple(past[0].shape[-1:])\\n\\n        zeros_key_val_shape = (\\n                tuple(past[0].shape[:-2]) + tuple([curr_length - window_length]) + tuple(past[0].shape[-1:])\\n        )\\n\\n        ones_mask = torch.ones(ones_key_val_shape)\\n        ones_mask = decay_mask * ones_mask.permute(0, 1, 2, 4, 3)\\n        ones_mask = ones_mask.permute(0, 1, 2, 4, 3)\\n\\n        window_mask = torch.cat((ones_mask, torch.zeros(zeros_key_val_shape)), dim=-2).to(device)\\n    else:\\n        window_mask = torch.ones_like(past[0]).to(device)\\n\\n    # accumulate perturbations for num_iterations\\n    loss_per_iter = []\\n    new_accumulated_hidden = None\\n    for i in range(num_iterations):\\n        curr_perturbation = [\\n            to_var(torch.from_numpy(p_), requires_grad=True, device=device) for p_ in grad_accumulator\\n        ]\\n\\n        # Compute hidden using perturbed past\\n        perturbed_past = list(map(add, past, curr_perturbation))\\n        _, _, _, curr_length, _ = curr_perturbation[0].shape\\n        all_logits, _, all_hidden = model(last, past=perturbed_past)\\n        hidden = all_hidden[-1]\\n        new_accumulated_hidden = accumulated_hidden + torch.sum(hidden, dim=1).detach()\\n        logits = all_logits[:, -1, :]\\n        probs = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1)\\n\\n        loss = 0.0\\n        loss_list = []\\n\\n        ce_loss = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()\\n        curr_unpert_past = unpert_past\\n        curr_probs = torch.unsqueeze(probs, dim=1)\\n        wte = model.resize_token_embeddings()\\n        for _ in range(horizon_length):\\n            inputs_embeds = torch.matmul(curr_probs, wte.weight.data)\\n            _, curr_unpert_past, curr_all_hidden = model(past=curr_unpert_past, inputs_embeds=inputs_embeds)\\n            curr_hidden = curr_all_hidden[-1]\\n            new_accumulated_hidden = new_accumulated_hidden + torch.sum(curr_hidden, dim=1)\\n\\n        prediction = classifier(new_accumulated_hidden / (curr_length + 1 + horizon_length))\\n\\n        label = torch.tensor(prediction.shape[0] * [class_label], device=device, dtype=torch.long)\\n        discrim_loss = ce_loss(prediction, label)\\n        loss += discrim_loss\\n        loss_list.append(discrim_loss)\\n\\n        if kl_scale > 0.0:\\n            unpert_probs = F.softmax(unpert_logits[:, -1, :], dim=-1)\\n            unpert_probs = unpert_probs + SMALL_CONST * (unpert_probs <= SMALL_CONST).float().to(device).detach()\\n            correction = SMALL_CONST * (probs <= SMALL_CONST).float().to(device).detach()\\n            corrected_probs = probs + correction.detach()\\n            kl_loss = kl_scale * ((corrected_probs * (corrected_probs / unpert_probs).log()).sum())\\n            loss += kl_loss\\n\\n        loss_per_iter.append(loss.data.cpu().numpy())\\n\\n        # compute gradients\\n        loss.backward()\\n\\n        # calculate gradient norms\\n        grad_norms = [\\n            (torch.norm(p_.grad * window_mask) + SMALL_CONST) for index, p_ in enumerate(curr_perturbation)\\n        ]\\n\\n        # normalize gradients\\n        grad = [\\n            -stepsize * (p_.grad * window_mask / grad_norms[index] ** gamma).data.cpu().numpy()\\n            for index, p_ in enumerate(curr_perturbation)\\n        ]\\n\\n        # accumulate gradient\\n        grad_accumulator = list(map(add, grad, grad_accumulator))\\n\\n        # reset gradients, just to make sure\\n        for p_ in curr_perturbation:\\n            p_.grad.data.zero_()\\n\\n        # removing past from the graph\\n        new_past = []\\n        for p_ in past:\\n            new_past.append(p_.detach())\\n        past = new_past\\n\\n    # apply the accumulated perturbations to the past\\n    grad_accumulator = [to_var(torch.from_numpy(p_), requires_grad=True, device=device) for p_ in grad_accumulator]\\n    pert_past = list(map(add, past, grad_accumulator))\\n\\n    return pert_past, new_accumulated_hidden, grad_norms, loss_per_iter\\n\\n\\ndef generate_text_pplm(\\n        model,\\n        context=None,\\n        past=None,\\n        device=\\"cuda\\",\\n        perturb=True,\\n        classifier=None,\\n        class_label=None,\\n        length=100,\\n        stepsize=0.02,\\n        temperature=1.0,\\n        top_k=10,\\n        sample=False,\\n        num_iterations=3,\\n        grad_length=10000,\\n        horizon_length=1,\\n        window_length=0,\\n        decay=False,\\n        gamma=1.5,\\n        gm_scale=0.9,\\n        kl_scale=0.01,\\n        repetition_penalty=1.0,\\n):\\n    output_so_far = None\\n    if context:\\n        context_t = torch.tensor(context, device=device, dtype=torch.long)\\n        while len(context_t.shape) < 2:\\n            context_t = context_t.unsqueeze(0)\\n        output_so_far = context_t\\n\\n    grad_norms = None\\n    last = None\\n    unpert_discrim_loss = 0\\n    loss_in_time = []\\n    for i in trange(length, desc=\'Generating with PPLM\', disable=True):\\n\\n        # Get past/probs for current output, except for last word\\n        # Note that GPT takes 2 inputs: past + current_token\\n\\n        # run model forward to obtain unperturbed\\n        if past is None and output_so_far is not None:\\n            last = output_so_far[:, -1:]\\n            if output_so_far.shape[1] > 1:\\n                _, past, _ = model(output_so_far[:, :-1])\\n\\n        unpert_logits, unpert_past, unpert_all_hidden = model(output_so_far)\\n        unpert_last_hidden = unpert_all_hidden[-1]\\n\\n        # check if we are abowe grad max length\\n        if i >= grad_length:\\n            current_stepsize = stepsize * 0\\n        else:\\n            current_stepsize = stepsize\\n\\n        # modify the past if necessary\\n        if not perturb or num_iterations == 0:\\n            pert_past = past\\n\\n        else:\\n            accumulated_hidden = unpert_last_hidden[:, :-1, :]\\n            accumulated_hidden = torch.sum(accumulated_hidden, dim=1)\\n\\n            if past is not None:\\n                pert_past, _, grad_norms, loss_this_iter = perturb_past(\\n                    past,\\n                    model,\\n                    last,\\n                    unpert_past=unpert_past,\\n                    unpert_logits=unpert_logits,\\n                    accumulated_hidden=accumulated_hidden,\\n                    grad_norms=grad_norms,\\n                    stepsize=current_stepsize,\\n                    classifier=classifier,\\n                    class_label=class_label,\\n                    num_iterations=num_iterations,\\n                    horizon_length=horizon_length,\\n                    window_length=window_length,\\n                    decay=decay,\\n                    gamma=gamma,\\n                    kl_scale=kl_scale,\\n                    device=device,\\n                )\\n                loss_in_time.append(loss_this_iter)\\n            else:\\n                pert_past = past\\n\\n        pert_logits, past, pert_all_hidden = model(last, past=pert_past)\\n        pert_logits = pert_logits[:, -1, :] / temperature  # + SMALL_CONST\\n\\n        for token_idx in set(output_so_far[0].tolist()):\\n            if pert_logits[0, token_idx] < 0:\\n                pert_logits[0, token_idx] *= repetition_penalty\\n            else:\\n                pert_logits[0, token_idx] /= repetition_penalty\\n\\n        pert_probs = F.softmax(pert_logits, dim=-1)\\n\\n        if classifier is not None:\\n            ce_loss = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()\\n            prediction = classifier(torch.mean(unpert_last_hidden, dim=1))\\n            label = torch.tensor([class_label], device=device, dtype=torch.long)\\n            unpert_discrim_loss = ce_loss(prediction, label)\\n            # print(\\"unperturbed discrim loss\\", unpert_discrim_loss.data.cpu().numpy())\\n        else:\\n            unpert_discrim_loss = 0\\n\\n        # Fuse the modified model and original model\\n        if perturb:\\n\\n            unpert_probs = F.softmax(unpert_logits[:, -1, :], dim=-1)\\n\\n            pert_probs = (pert_probs ** gm_scale) * (unpert_probs ** (1 - gm_scale))  # + SMALL_CONST\\n            pert_probs = top_k_filter(pert_probs, k=top_k, probs=True)  # + SMALL_CONST\\n\\n            # rescale\\n            if torch.sum(pert_probs) <= 1:\\n                pert_probs = pert_probs / torch.sum(pert_probs)\\n\\n        else:\\n            pert_logits = top_k_filter(pert_logits, k=top_k)  # + SMALL_CONST\\n            pert_probs = F.softmax(pert_logits, dim=-1)\\n\\n        # sample or greedy\\n        if sample:\\n            last = torch.multinomial(pert_probs, num_samples=1)\\n\\n        else:\\n            _, last = torch.topk(pert_probs, k=1, dim=-1)\\n\\n        # update context/output_so_far appending the new token\\n        output_so_far = last if output_so_far is None else torch.cat((output_so_far, last), dim=1)\\n\\n        # print(tokenizer.decode(output_so_far.tolist()[0]))\\n\\n    return output_so_far, unpert_discrim_loss, loss_in_time\\n\\n\\nclass PPLMGeneration(Pipeline):\\n    def __init__(self,\\n                 discrim: str,\\n                 seed=0,\\n                 **kwargs):\\n        # Set random seed\\n        torch.manual_seed(seed)\\n        np.random.seed(seed)\\n\\n        pretrained_model = DISCRIMINATOR_MODELS_PARAMS[discrim][\\"pretrained_model\\"]\\n        print(\\"discrim = {}, pretrained_model set \\" \\"to discriminator\'s = {}\\".format(discrim, pretrained_model))\\n\\n        # load pretrained model\\n        model = GPT2LMHeadModel.from_pretrained(pretrained_model, output_hidden_states=True)\\n        model.eval()\\n\\n        # load tokenizer\\n        tokenizer = GPT2Tokenizer.from_pretrained(pretrained_model)\\n\\n        # Freeze GPT-2 weights\\n        for param in model.parameters():\\n            param.requires_grad = False\\n\\n        super().__init__(model=model, tokenizer=tokenizer, **kwargs)\\n\\n        # Additional setup after creating model and tokenizer\\n        self.discrim = discrim\\n        classifier = get_classifier(self.discrim, self.device)\\n        self.classifier = classifier\\n\\n    # Default parameters correspond to those in the PPLM paper for the toxicity discriminative model\\n    # Others (such as sampling) taken from https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/tree/master/examples/pplm\\n    def __call__(self,\\n                 cond_text=\'\',\\n                 num_samples=1,\\n                 class_label=-1,\\n                 length=20,\\n                 stepsize=0.02,\\n                 temperature=1.0,\\n                 top_k=10,\\n                 sample=True,\\n                 num_iterations=10,\\n                 grad_length=10000,\\n                 horizon_length=1,\\n                 window_length=0,\\n                 decay=False,\\n                 gamma=1.0,\\n                 gm_scale=0.9,\\n                 kl_scale=0.01,\\n                 repetition_penalty=1.0,\\n                 clean_up_tokenization_spaces=True,\\n                 include_context_in_generation=False):\\n        # Tokenize text\\n        tokenized_cond_text = self.tokenizer.encode(self.tokenizer.bos_token + cond_text)\\n\\n        class_id = get_class_id(self.discrim, class_label)\\n\\n        pert_gen_tok_texts = []\\n        for i in range(num_samples):\\n            pert_gen_tok_text, discrim_loss, loss_in_time = generate_text_pplm(\\n                model=self.model,\\n                context=tokenized_cond_text,\\n                device=self.device,\\n                perturb=True,\\n                classifier=self.classifier,\\n                class_label=class_id,\\n                length=length,\\n                stepsize=stepsize,\\n                temperature=temperature,\\n                top_k=top_k,\\n                sample=sample,\\n                num_iterations=num_iterations,\\n                grad_length=grad_length,\\n                horizon_length=horizon_length,\\n                window_length=window_length,\\n                decay=decay,\\n                gamma=gamma,\\n                gm_scale=gm_scale,\\n                kl_scale=kl_scale,\\n                repetition_penalty=repetition_penalty,\\n            )\\n            pert_gen_tok_texts.append(pert_gen_tok_text)\\n\\n        decode_start_idx = 0 if include_context_in_generation else len(tokenized_cond_text)\\n        pert_gen_tok_texts = [x[0, decode_start_idx:].tolist() for x in pert_gen_tok_texts]\\n        pert_gen_texts = [\\n            self.tokenizer.decode(pert_gen_tok_text, clean_up_tokenization_spaces=clean_up_tokenization_spaces)\\n            for pert_gen_tok_text in pert_gen_tok_texts\\n        ]\\n\\n        return pert_gen_texts\\n"]}]\n\n# Workflow\n1. 2025-09-18 23:36:24,412 - llm.llm_def - DEBUG - OpenAI query completed in 46.39s Response: ```python
"""
This module implements the core Plug and Play Language Model (PPLM) technique,
which is a decoding-based detoxification method that steers a language model’s
generation away from toxic content by perturbing its hidden states using gradient
information from a classifier. The primary functions are perturb_past and generate_text_pplm,
which modify the model’s past activations during token generation. All configurable options,
such as stepsize, top_k filtering, temperature, and perturbation iterations, are exposed as
parameters in the generate_text method. Users can instantiate the PPLM class by providing a
pretrained language model and an optional classifier and then call its generate method to produce
controlled text generation. The code is self-contained and relies on standard libraries (numpy,
torch, transformers) and includes a test block to demonstrate usage.
"""

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.autograd import Variable
from tqdm import trange
from transformers import GPT2LMHeadModel, GPT2Tokenizer

# Small constants used for numerical stability.
SMALL_CONST = 1e-15
BIG_CONST = 1e10

def to_var(x: torch.Tensor, requires_grad: bool = False, device: str = "cuda") -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Wraps a torch.Tensor in a Variable and sends it to the specified device.
    Args:
        x (torch.Tensor): The tensor to wrap.
        requires_grad (bool): Whether gradient tracking is needed.
        device (str): The target device ('cuda' or 'cpu').
    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: The tensor placed on the target device with the required properties.
    """
    if torch.cuda.is_available() and device == "cuda":
        x = x.cuda()
    else:
        x = x.to(device)
    return Variable(x, requires_grad=requires_grad)

def top_k_filter(logits: torch.Tensor, k: int, probs: bool = False) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Masks logits, keeping only the top k values while setting the rest to a very small value.
    Args:
        logits (torch.Tensor): The logits tensor.
        k (int): The number of top entries to retain.
        probs (bool): Whether the logits represent probabilities.
    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: The filtered logits tensor.
    """
    if k == 0:
        return logits
    else:
        values = torch.topk(logits, k)[0]
        batch_mins = values[:, -1].view(-1, 1).expand_as(logits)
        if probs:
            return torch.where(logits < batch_mins, torch.zeros_like(logits), logits)
        return torch.where(logits < batch_mins, torch.full_like(logits, -BIG_CONST), logits)

def perturb_past(
    past: list,
    model: torch.nn.Module,
    last: torch.Tensor,
    unpert_past: torch.Tensor = None,
    unpert_logits: torch.Tensor = None,
    accumulated_hidden: torch.Tensor = None,
    grad_norms: list = None,
    stepsize: float = 0.01,
    classifier: torch.nn.Module = None,
    class_label: int = None,
    num_iterations: int = 3,
    horizon_length: int = 1,
    window_length: int = 0,
    decay: bool = False,
    gamma: float = 1.5,
    kl_scale: float = 0.01,
    device: str = "cuda",
) -> (list, torch.Tensor, list, list):
    """
    Perturbs the past hidden states in order to steer the language model's generation.
    Args:
        past (list): The past hidden states from the language model.
        model (torch.nn.Module): The language model.
        last (torch.Tensor): The last token tensor.
        unpert_past (torch.Tensor, optional): The unperturbed past states.
        unpert_logits (torch.Tensor, optional): The logits from the unperturbed model.
        accumulated_hidden (torch.Tensor, optional): Accumulated hidden states.
        grad_norms (list, optional): List to store gradient norms.
        stepsize (float): Step size for gradient update.
        classifier (torch.nn.Module, optional): Classifier for attribute guidance.
        class_label (int, optional): Target class label.
        num_iterations (int): Number of gradient ascent iterations.
        horizon_length (int): How many steps ahead to consider.
        window_length (int): Length of the past window to perturb.
        decay (bool): Whether to apply decay weighting.
        gamma (float): Exponential decay factor.
        kl_scale (float): Weight for the KL-control loss term.
        device (str): The device to run computations on.
    Returns:
        A tuple of (perturbed past, new accumulated hidden, gradient norms, loss per iteration).
    """
    # Initialize gradient accumulator
    grad_accumulator = [np.zeros(p.shape, dtype="float32") for p in past]
    if accumulated_hidden is None:
        accumulated_hidden = 0

    if decay and window_length > 0:
        decay_mask = torch.arange(0.0, 1.0 + SMALL_CONST, 1.0 / window_length)[1:].to(device)
    else:
        decay_mask = 1.0

    # Create a window mask for perturbations if a window is defined.
    _, _, _, curr_length, _ = past[0].shape
    if curr_length > window_length and window_length > 0:
        ones_key_val_shape = past[0].shape[:-2] + (window_length,) + past[0].shape[-1:]
        zeros_key_val_shape = past[0].shape[:-2] + (curr_length - window_length,) + past[0].shape[-1:]
        ones_mask = torch.ones(ones_key_val_shape, device=device)
        ones_mask = decay_mask * ones_mask.permute(0, 1, 2, 4, 3)
        ones_mask = ones_mask.permute(0, 1, 2, 4, 3)
        window_mask = torch.cat((ones_mask, torch.zeros(zeros_key_val_shape, device=device)), dim=-2)
    else:
        window_mask = torch.ones_like(past[0], device=device)

    loss_per_iter = []
    new_accumulated_hidden = None
    for _ in range(num_iterations):
        curr_perturbation = [to_var(torch.from_numpy(p_), requires_grad=True, device=device)
                             for p_ in grad_accumulator]
        # Compute perturbed past
        perturbed_past = [p + delta for p, delta in zip(past, curr_perturbation)]
        _, _, all_hidden = model(last, past=perturbed_past)
        hidden = all_hidden[-1]
        new_accumulated_hidden = accumulated_hidden + torch.sum(hidden, dim=1).detach()
        logits = model(last, past=perturbed_past)[0][:, -1, :]
        probs = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1)

        loss = 0.0
        ce_loss = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
        # Look ahead horizon
        curr_unpert_past = unpert_past
        curr_probs = torch.unsqueeze(probs, dim=1)
        wte = model.get_input_embeddings()
        for _ in range(horizon_length):
            inputs_embeds = torch.matmul(curr_probs, wte.weight.data)
            _, curr_unpert_past, curr_all_hidden = model(inputs_embeds=inputs_embeds, past=curr_unpert_past)
            curr_hidden = curr_all_hidden[-1]
            new_accumulated_hidden = new_accumulated_hidden + torch.sum(curr_hidden, dim=1)
        if classifier is not None:
            prediction = classifier(new_accumulated_hidden / (curr_length + 1 + horizon_length))
            label = torch.full((prediction.shape[0],), class_label, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            discrim_loss = ce_loss(prediction, label)
            loss += discrim_loss
        if kl_scale > 0.0 and unpert_logits is not None:
            unpert_probs = F.softmax(unpert_logits[:, -1, :], dim=-1)
            unpert_probs = unpert_probs + SMALL_CONST * (unpert_probs <= SMALL_CONST).float().to(device)
            correction = SMALL_CONST * (probs <= SMALL_CONST).float().to(device)
            corrected_probs = probs + correction.detach()
            kl_loss = kl_scale * (corrected_probs * (corrected_probs / unpert_probs).log()).sum()
            loss += kl_loss

        loss_per_iter.append(loss.item())

        loss.backward()
        grad_norms = [(torch.norm(p_.grad * window_mask) + SMALL_CONST) for p_ in curr_perturbation]
        grad = [(-stepsize * (p_.grad * window_mask / (grad_norm ** gamma)).data).cpu().numpy()
                for grad_norm, p_ in zip(grad_norms, curr_perturbation)]
        grad_accumulator = [g + acc for g, acc in zip(grad, grad_accumulator)]
        for p_ in curr_perturbation:
            p_.grad.data.zero_()
        past = [p_.detach() for p_ in past]
    grad_accumulator = [to_var(torch.from_numpy(p_), requires_grad=True, device=device) for p_ in grad_accumulator]
    pert_past = [p + delta for p, delta in zip(past, grad_accumulator)]
    return pert_past, new_accumulated_hidden, grad_norms, loss_per_iter

def generate_text_pplm(
    model: torch.nn.Module,
    context: list = None,
    past: list = None,
    device: str = "cuda",
    perturb: bool = True,
    classifier: torch.nn.Module = None,
    class_label: int = 0,
    length: int = 20,
    stepsize: float = 0.02,
    temperature: float = 1.0,
    top_k: int = 10,
    sample: bool = True,
    num_iterations: int = 3,
    grad_length: int = 10000,
    horizon_length: int = 1,
    window_length: int = 0,
    decay: bool = False,
    gamma: float = 1.0,
    gm_scale: float = 0.9,
    kl_scale: float = 0.01,
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
) -> (torch.Tensor, float, list):
    """
    Generates text using the PPLM approach by perturbing the hidden states of the model.
    Args:
        model (torch.nn.Module): The pretrained language model.
        context (list, optional): Tokenized context (list of token ids).
        past (list, optional): Cached past hidden states.
        device (str): Device to run the computations on.
        perturb (bool): Whether to apply perturbations.
        classifier (torch.nn.Module, optional): Classifier guiding the perturbations.
        class_label (int): Target label for the classifier.
        length (int): Number of tokens to generate.
        stepsize (float): Step size for perturbation updates.
        temperature (float): Temperature parameter for scaling logits.
        top_k (int): Top-k filtering value.
        sample (bool): Whether to sample or use greedy decoding.
        num_iterations (int): Iterations for hidden state perturbation.
        grad_length (int): Maximum number of tokens to apply gradients.
        horizon_length (int): Look-ahead horizon length.
        window_length (int): Length of the perturbation window.
        decay (bool): Whether to apply decay to the perturbations.
        gamma (float): Decay factor.
        gm_scale (float): Geometric mean scale factor between perturbed and unperturbed probabilities.
        kl_scale (float): Scale of KL divergence for regularization.
        repetition_penalty (float): Repetition penalty factor.
    Returns:
        A tuple of (generated tokens tensor, unperturbed classifier loss, list of per-iteration losses).
    """
    output_so_far = torch.tensor(context, device=device, dtype=torch.long).unsqueeze(0) if context else None
    grad_norms = None
    last = None
    unpert_discrim_loss = 0
    loss_in_time = []

    for i in trange(length, desc='Generating with PPLM', leave=False):
        # Get past and last token information.
        if past is None and output_so_far is not None:
            last = output_so_far[:, -1:]
            if output_so_far.shape[1] > 1:
                _, past, _ = model(output_so_far[:, :-1])
        unpert_logits, unpert_past, unpert_all_hidden = model(output_so_far)
        unpert_last_hidden = unpert_all_hidden[-1]
        current_stepsize = stepsize if i < grad_length else 0.0

        if not perturb or num_iterations == 0:
            pert_past = past
        else:
            accumulated_hidden = torch.sum(unpert_last_hidden[:, :-1, :], dim=1)
            pert_past, _, grad_norms, loss_this_iter = perturb_past(
                past,
                model,
                last,
                unpert_past=unpert_past,
                unpert_logits=unpert_logits,
                accumulated_hidden=accumulated_hidden,
                grad_norms=grad_norms,
                stepsize=current_stepsize,
                classifier=classifier,
                class_label=class_label,
                num_iterations=num_iterations,
                horizon_length=horizon_length,
                window_length=window_length,
                decay=decay,
                gamma=gamma,
                kl_scale=kl_scale,
                device=device,
            )
            loss_in_time.append(loss_this_iter)

        pert_logits, past, pert_all_hidden = model(last, past=pert_past)
        pert_logits = pert_logits[:, -1, :] / temperature

        # Apply repetition penalty.
        for token_idx in set(output_so_far[0].tolist()):
            if pert_logits[0, token_idx] < 0:
                pert_logits[0, token_idx] *= repetition_penalty
            else:
                pert_logits[0, token_idx] /= repetition_penalty

        pert_probs = F.softmax(pert_logits, dim=-1)

        if classifier is not None:
            # Calculate unperturbed classifier loss for logging.
            ce_loss = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
            prediction = classifier(torch.mean(unpert_last_hidden, dim=1))
            label = torch.tensor([class_label], device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            unpert_discrim_loss = ce_loss(prediction, label).item()
        else:
            unpert_discrim_loss = 0

        # Fuse perturbed and unperturbed probabilities.
        if perturb:
            unpert_probs = F.softmax(unpert_logits[:, -1, :], dim=-1)
            pert_probs = (pert_probs ** gm_scale) * (unpert_probs ** (1 - gm_scale))
            pert_probs = top_k_filter(pert_probs, k=top_k, probs=True)
            if torch.sum(pert_probs) <= 1:
                pert_probs = pert_probs / torch.sum(pert_probs)
        else:
            pert_logits = top_k_filter(pert_logits, k=top_k)
            pert_probs = F.softmax(pert_logits, dim=-1)

        # Sample from the probability distribution or select the highest token.
        if sample:
            last = torch.multinomial(pert_probs, num_samples=1)
        else:
            _, last = torch.topk(pert_probs, k=1, dim=-1)
        output_so_far = last if output_so_far is None else torch.cat((output_so_far, last), dim=1)
    return output_so_far, unpert_discrim_loss, loss_in_time

class PPLM:
    """
    A class encapsulating the Plug and Play Language Model (PPLM) technique.
    It perturbs the hidden states of a language model during generation to steer the output
    towards desired attributes (e.g., non-toxicity) using gradient guidance from a classifier.
    """

    def __init__(self, model: torch.nn.Module, tokenizer: GPT2Tokenizer, classifier: torch.nn.Module = None, device: str = "cuda") -> None:
        """
        Initializes the PPLM instance.
        Args:
            model (torch.nn.Module): The pretrained language model.
            tokenizer (GPT2Tokenizer): Tokenizer corresponding to the language model.
            classifier (torch.nn.Module, optional): Classifier used for guiding generation.
            device (str): The device for computation ('cuda' or 'cpu').
        """
        self.model = model.to(device)
        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
        self.classifier = classifier
        self.device = device
        # Freeze model parameters.
        for param in self.model.parameters():
            param.requires_grad = False

    def generate(self,
                 cond_text: str,
                 num_tokens: int = 20,
                 class_label: int = 0,
                 stepsize: float = 0.02,
                 temperature: float = 1.0,
                 top_k: int = 10,
                 sample: bool = True,
                 num_iterations: int = 3,
                 horizon_length: int = 1,
                 window_length: int = 0,
                 decay: bool = False,
                 gamma: float = 1.0,
                 gm_scale: float = 0.9,
                 kl_scale: float = 0.01,
                 repetition_penalty: float = 1.0) -> str:
        """
        Generates text using the PPLM technique.
        Args:
            cond_text (str): The conditioning text prompt.
            num_tokens (int): Number of tokens to generate.
            class_label (int): Target class label for the classifier.
            stepsize (float): Step size for gradient update.
            temperature (float): Temperature for scaling logits.
            top_k (int): Top-k sampling filter parameter.
            sample (bool): Whether to sample or use greedy decoding.
            num_iterations (int): Number of perturbation iterations.
            horizon_length (int): Look-ahead horizon length.
            window_length (int): Length of the hidden state window to perturb.
            decay (bool): Whether to apply decay to the perturbation.
            gamma (float): Decay factor.
            gm_scale (float): Geometric mean scaling factor.
            kl_scale (float): KL divergence scale factor.
            repetition_penalty (float): Repetition penalty factor.
        Returns:
            str: The generated text.
        """
        tokenized_cond = self.tokenizer.encode(self.tokenizer.bos_token + cond_text)
        generated_tokens, _, _ = generate_text_pplm(
            model=self.model,
            context=tokenized_cond,
            device=self.device,
            perturb=True,
            classifier=self.classifier,
            class_label=class_label,
            length=num_tokens,
            stepsize=stepsize,
            temperature=temperature,
            top_k=top_k,
            sample=sample,
            num_iterations=num_iterations,
            horizon_length=horizon_length,
            window_length=window_length,
            decay=decay,
            gamma=gamma,
            gm_scale=gm_scale,
            kl_scale=kl_scale,
            repetition_penalty=repetition_penalty,
        )
        # Exclude the conditioning context if present.
        decode_start_idx = len(tokenized_cond)
        generated_text = self.tokenizer.decode(generated_tokens[0, decode_start_idx:], clean_up_tokenization_spaces=True)
        return generated_text

# TEST BLOCK
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Using the GPT-2 small model for demonstration purposes.
    device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
    model_name = "gpt2"
    model = GPT2LMHeadModel.from_pretrained(model_name, output_hidden_states=True)
    tokenizer = GPT2Tokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name)
    
    # Optionally, define a dummy classifier that returns uniform logits.
    class DummyClassifier(torch.nn.Module):
        def __init__(self, num_classes: int = 2):
            super(DummyClassifier, self).__init__()
            self.linear = torch.nn.Linear(768, num_classes)
        def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
            # x assumed to be [batch_size, hidden_dim]; output shape [batch_size, num_classes]
            return self.linear(x.mean(dim=1, keepdim=True)).squeeze(1)
    
    # For testing, we do not use a real classifier so set classifier to None.
    classifier = None  # or DummyClassifier() if testing with a dummy classifier

    pplm = PPLM(model=model, tokenizer=tokenizer, classifier=classifier, device=device)
    prompt = "Once upon a time"
    generated_text = pplm.generate(cond_text=prompt, num_tokens=30, class_label=0)
    print("Prompt:", prompt)
    print("Generated Text:", generated_text)
```

```
This module encapsulates the Plug and Play Language Model (PPLM) technique designed to steer a language model toward generating non-toxic text. The technique operates by perturbing the model’s past hidden states using gradient information, and its core functions include perturb_past and generate_text_pplm. The module allows configurable parameters such as stepsize, top-k filtering, temperature scaling, and the number of perturbation iterations. The PPLM class integrates the method with a pretrained language model and an optional classifier, making it easy to generate controlled text output. A test block at the end demonstrates how to instantiate and use the PPLM class with GPT-2 and a sample prompt. This self-contained implementation is intended for research and development purposes in controlled text generation.

The final code block must be clean, well-documented, and easy for others to understand and run.

# Input
1. Abstract of the paper RealToxicityPrompts: Evaluating Neural Toxic Degeneration in Language  Models:
Pretrained neural language models (LMs) are prone to generating racist,
sexist, or otherwise toxic language which hinders their safe deployment. We
investigate the extent to which pretrained LMs can be prompted to generate
toxic language, and the effectiveness of controllable text generation
algorithms at preventing such toxic degeneration. We create and release
RealToxicityPrompts, a dataset of 100K naturally occurring, sentence-level
prompts derived from a large corpus of English web text, paired with toxicity
scores from a widely-used toxicity classifier. Using RealToxicityPrompts, we
find that pretrained LMs can degenerate into toxic text even from seemingly
innocuous prompts. We empirically assess several controllable generation
methods, and find that while data- or compute-intensive methods (e.g., adaptive
pretraining on non-toxic data) are more effective at steering away from
toxicity than simpler solutions (e.g., banning "bad" words), no current method
is failsafe against neural toxic degeneration. To pinpoint the potential cause
of such persistent toxic degeneration, we analyze two web text corpora used to
pretrain several LMs (including GPT-2; Radford et. al, 2019), and find a
significant amount of offensive, factually unreliable, and otherwise toxic
content. Our work provides a test bed for evaluating toxic generations by LMs
and stresses the need for better data selection processes for pretraining.

2. Technical Concept Definition from the paper RealToxicityPrompts: Evaluating Neural Toxic Degeneration in Language  Models:
{
  "name": "Toxicity Classifier Gradient Update",
  "description": "Uses gradients from a toxicity classifier to update the model's hidden representations during generation, steering the output towards less toxic text."
}

\"class_vocab\": {\"non_toxic\": 0, \"toxic\": 1},\n        \"default_class\": 0,\n        \"pretrained_model\": \"gpt2-medium\",\n    }\n}\n\n\ndef to_var(x, requires_grad=False, volatile=False, device=\"cuda\"):\n    if torch.cuda.is_available() and device == \"cuda\":\n        x = x.cuda()\n    elif device != \"cuda\":\n        x = x.to(device)\n    return Variable(x, requires_grad=requires_grad, volatile=volatile)\n\n\ndef top_k_filter(logits, k, probs=False):\n    \"\"\"\n    Masks everything but the k top entries as -infinity (1e10).\n    Used to mask logits such that e^-infinity -> 0 won't contribute to the\n    sum of the denominator.\n    \"\"\"\n    if k == 0:\n        return logits\n    else:\n        values = torch.topk(logits, k)[0]\n        batch_mins = values[:, -1].view(-1, 1).expand_as(logits)\n        if probs:\n            return torch.where(logits < batch_mins, torch.ones_like(logits) * 0.0, logits)\n        return torch.where(logits < batch_mins, torch.ones_like(logits) * -BIG_CONST, logits)\n\n\ndef get_classifier(name: str, device: str) -> ClassificationHead:\n    params = DISCRIMINATOR_MODELS_PARAMS[name]\n    classifier = ClassificationHead(class_size=params[\"class_size\"], embed_size=params[\"embed_size\"]).to(device)\n    if \"url\" in params:\n        resolved_archive_file =    print(\"available values are: {}\".format(params[\"class_vocab\"]))\n            print(\"using default class {}\".format(label_id))\n    elif isinstance(class_label, int):\n        if class_label in set(params[\"class_vocab\"].values()):\n            label_id = class_label\n        else:\n            label_id = params[\"default_class\"]\n            print(\"class_label {} not in class_vocab\".format(class_label))\n            print(\"available values are: {}\".format(params[\"class_vocab\"]))\n            print(\"using default class {}\".format(label_id))\n    else:\n        label_id = params[\"default_class\"]\n    return label_id\n\n\ndef perturb_past(\n        past,\n        model,\n        last,\n        unpert_past=None,\n        unpert_logits=None,\n        accumulated_hidden=None,\n        grad_norms=None,\n        stepsize=0.01,\n        classifier=None,\n        class_label=None,\n        num_iterations=3,\n        horizon_length=1,\n        window_length=0,\n        decay=False,\n        gamma=1.5,\n        kl_scale=0.01,\n        device=\"cuda\",\n):\n    # Generate inital perturbed past\n    grad_accumulator = [(np.zeros(p.shape).astype(\"float32\")) for p in past]\n\n    if accumulated_hidden is None:\n        accumulated_hidden = 0\n\n    if decay:\n        decay_mask = torch.arange(0.0, 1.0 + SMALL_CONST, 1.0 / (window_length))[1:]\n    else:\n        decay_mask = 1.0\n\n    # Generate a mask is gradient perturbated is based on a past window\n    _, _, _, curr_length, _ = past[0].shape\n\n    if curr_length > window_length and window_length > 0:\n        ones_key_val_shape = tuple(past[0].shape[:-2]) + tuple([window_length]) + tuple(past[0].shape[-1:])\n\n        zeros_key_val_shape = (\n                tuple(past[0].shape[:-2]) + tuple([curr_length - window_length]) + tuple(past[0].shape[-1:])\n        )\n\n        ones_mask = torch.ones(ones_key_val_shape)\n        ones_mask = decay_mask * ones_mask.permute(0, 1, 2, 4, 3)\n        ones_mask = ones_mask.permute(0, 1, 2, 4, 3)\n\n        window_mask = torch.cat((ones_mask, torch.zeros(zeros_key_val_shape)), dim=-2).to(device)\n    else:\n        window_mask = torch.ones_like(past[0]).to(device)\n\n    # accumulate perturbations for num_iterations\n    loss_per_iter = []\n    new_accumulated_hidden = None\n    for i in range(num_iterations):\n        curr_perturbation = [\n            to_var(torch.from_numpy(p_), requires_grad=True, device=device) for p_ in grad_accumulator\n        ]\n\n        # Compute hidden using perturbed past\n        perturbed_past = list(map(add, past, curr_perturbation))\n        _, _, _, curr_length, _ = curr_perturbation[0].shape\n        all_logits, _, all_hidden = model(last, past=perturbed_past)\n        hidden = all_hidden[-1]\n        new_accumulated_hidden = accumulated_hidden + torch.sum(hidden, dim=1).detach()\n        logits = all_logits[:, -1, :]\n        probs = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1)\n\n        loss = 0.0\n        loss_list = []\n\n        ce_loss = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()\n        curr_unpert_past = unpert_past\n        curr_probs = torch.unsqueeze(probs, dim=1)\n        wte = model.resize_token_embeddings()\n        for _ in range(horizon_length):\n            inputs_embeds = torch.matmul(curr_probs, wte.weight.data)\n            _, curr_unpert_past, curr_all_hidden = model(past=curr_unpert_past, inputs_embeds=inputs_embeds)\n            curr_hidden = curr_all_hidden[-1]\n            new_accumulated_hidden = new_accumulated_hidden + torch.sum(curr_hidden, dim=1)\n\n        prediction = classifier(new_accumulated_hidden / (curr_length + 1 + horizon_length))\n\n        label = torch.tensor(prediction.shape[0] * [class_label], device=device, dtype=torch.long)\n        discrim_loss = ce_loss(prediction, label)\n        loss += discrim_loss\n        loss_list.append(discrim_loss)\n\n        if kl_scale > 0.0:\n            unpert_probs = F.softmax(unpert_logits[:, -1, :], dim=-1)\n            unpert_probs = unpert_probs + SMALL_CONST * (unpert_probs <= SMALL_CONST).float().to(device).detach()\n            correction = SMALL_CONST * (probs <= SMALL_CONST).float().to(device).detach()\n            corrected_probs = probs + correction.detach()\n            kl_loss = kl_scale * ((corrected_probs * (corrected_probs / unpert_probs).log()).sum())\n            loss += kl_loss\n\n        loss_per_iter.append(loss.data.cpu().numpy())\n\n        # compute gradients\n        loss.backward()\n\n        # calculate gradient norms\n        grad_norms = [\n            (torch.norm(p_.grad * window_mask) + SMALL_CONST) for index, p_ in enumerate(curr_perturbation)\n        ]\n\n        # normalize gradients\n        grad = [\n            -stepsize * (p_.grad * window_mask / grad_norms[index] ** gamma).data.cpu().numpy()\n            for index, p_ in enumerate(curr_perturbation)\n        ]\n\n        # accumulate gradient\n        grad_accumulator = list(map(add, grad, grad_accumulator))\n\n        # reset gradients, just to make sure\n        for p_ in curr_perturbation:\n            p_.grad.data.zero_()\n\n        # removing past from the graph\n        new_past = []\n        for p_ in past:\n            new_past.append(p_.detach())\n        past = new_past\n\n    # apply the accumulated perturbations to the past\n    grad_accumulator = [to_var(torch.from_numpy(p_), requires_grad=True, device=device) for p_ in grad_accumulator]\n    pert_past = list(map(add, past, grad_accumulator))\n\n    return pert_past, new_accumulated_hidden, grad_norms, loss_per_iter\n\n\ndef generate_text_pplm(\n        model,\n        context=None,\n        past=None,\n        device=\"cuda\",\n        perturb=True,\n        classifier=None,\n        class_label=None,\n        length=100,\n        stepsize=0.02,\n        temperature=1.0,\n        top_k=10,\n        sample=False,\n        num_iterations=3,\n        grad_length=10000,\n        horizon_length=1,\n        window_length=0,\n        decay=False,\n        gamma=1.5,\n        gm_scale=0.9,\n        kl_scale=0.01,\n        repetition_penalty=1.0,\n):\n    output_so_far = None\n    if context:\n        context_t = torch.tensor(context, device=device, dtype=torch.long)\n        while len(context_t.shape) < 2:\n            context_t = context_t.unsqueeze(0)\n        output_so_far = context_t\n\n    grad_norms = None\n    last = None\n    unpert_discrim_loss = 0\n    loss_in_time = []\n    for i in trange(length, desc='Generating with PPLM', disable=True):\n\n        # Get past/probs for current output, except for last word\n        # Note that GPT takes 2 inputs: past + current_token\n\n        # run model forward to obtain unperturbed\n        if past is None and output_so_far is not None:\n            last = output_so_far[:, -1:]\n            if output_so_far.shape[1] > 1:\n                _, past, _ = model(output_so_far[:, :-1])\n\n        unpert_logits, unpert_past, unpert_all_hidden = model(output_so_far)\n        unpert_last_hidden = unpert_all_hidden[-1]\n\n        # check if we are abowe grad max length\n        if i >= grad_length:\n            current_stepsize = stepsize * 0\n        else:\n            current_stepsize = stepsize\n\n        # modify the past if necessary\n        if not perturb or num_iterations == 0:\n            pert_past = past\n\n        else:\n            accumulated_hidden = unpert_last_hidden[:, :-1, :]\n            accumulated_hidden = torch.sum(accumulated_hidden, dim=1)\n\n            if past is not None:\n                pert_past, _, grad_norms, loss_this_iter = perturb_past(\n                    past,\n                    model,\n                    last,\n                    unpert_past=unpert_past,\n                    unpert_logits=unpert_logits,\n                    accumulated_hidden=accumulated_hidden,\n                    grad_norms=grad_norms,\n                    stepsize=current_stepsize,\n                    classifier=classifier,\n                    class_label=class_label,\n                    num_iterations=num_iterations,\n                    horizon_length=horizon_length,\n                    window_length=window_length,\n                    decay=decay,\n                    gamma=gamma,\n                    kl_scale=kl_scale,\n                    device=device,\n                )\n                loss_in_time.append(loss_this_iter)\n            else:\n                pert_past = past\n\n        pert_logits, past, pert_all_hidden = model(last, past=pert_past)\n        pert_logits = pert_logits[:, -1, :] / temperature  # + SMALL_CONST\n\n        for token_idx in set(output_so_far[0].tolist()):\n            if pert_logits[0, token_idx] < 0:\n                pert_logits[0, token_idx] *= repetition_penalty\n            else:\n                pert_logits[0, token_idx] /= repetition_penalty\n\n        pert_probs = F.softmax(pert_logits, dim=-1)\n\n        if classifier is not None:\n            ce_loss = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()\n            prediction = classifier(torch.mean(unpert_last_hidden, dim=1))\n            label = torch.tensor([class_label], device=device, dtype=torch.long)\n            unpert_discrim_loss = ce_loss(prediction, label)\n            # print(\"unperturbed discrim loss\", unpert_discrim_loss.data.cpu().numpy())\n        else:\n            unpert_discrim_loss = 0\n\n        # Fuse the modified model and original model\n        if perturb:\n\n            unpert_probs = F.softmax(unpert_logits[:, -1, :], dim=-1)\n\n            pert_probs = (pert_probs ** gm_scale) * (unpert_probs ** (1 - gm_scale))  # + SMALL_CONST\n            pert_probs = top_k_filter(pert_probs, k=top_k, probs=True)  # + SMALL_CONST\n\n            # rescale\n            if torch.sum(pert_probs) <= 1:\n                pert_probs = pert_probs / torch.sum(pert_probs)\n\n        else:\n            pert_logits = top_k_filter(pert_logits, k=top_k)  # + SMALL_CONST\n            pert_probs = F.softmax(pert_logits, dim=-1)\n\n        # sample or greedy\n        if sample:\n            last = torch.multinomial(pert_probs, num_samples=1)\n\n        else:\n            _, last = torch.topk(pert_probs, k=1, dim=-1)\n\n        # update context/output_so_far appending the new token\n        output_so_far = last if output_so_far is None else torch.cat((output_so_far, last), dim=1)\n\n        # print(tokenizer.decode(output_so_far.tolist()[0]))\n\n    return output_so_far, unpert_discrim_loss, loss_in_time\n\n\nclass PPLMGeneration(Pipeline):\n    def __init__(self,\n                 discrim: str,\n                 seed=0,\n                 **kwargs):\n        # Set random seed\n        torch.manual_seed(seed)\n        np.random.seed(seed)\n\n        pretrained_model = DISCRIMINATOR_MODELS_PARAMS[discrim][\"pretrained_model\"]\n        print(\"discrim = {}, pretrained_model set \" \"to discriminator's = {}\".format(discrim, pretrained_model))\n\n        # load pretrained model\n        model = GPT2LMHeadModel.from_pretrained(pretrained_model, output_hidden_states=True)\n        model.eval()\n\n        # load tokenizer\n        tokenizer = GPT2Tokenizer.from_pretrained(pretrained_model)\n\n        # Freeze GPT-2 weights\n        for param in model.parameters():\n            param.requires_grad = False\n\n        super().__init__(model=model, tokenizer=tokenizer, **kwargs)\n\n        # Additional setup after creating model and tokenizer\n        self.discrim = discrim\n        classifier = get_classifier(self.discrim, self.device)\n        self.classifier = classifier\n\n    # Default parameters correspond to those in the PPLM paper for the toxicity discriminative model\n    # Others (such as sampling) taken from https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/tree/master/examples/pplm\n    def __call__(self,\n                 cond_text='',\n                 num_samples=1,\n                 class_label=-1,\n                 length=20,\n                 stepsize=0.02,\n                 temperature=1.0,\n                 top_k=10,\n                 sample=True,\n                 num_iterations=10,\n                 grad_length=10000,\n                 horizon_length=1,\n                 window_length=0,\n                 decay=False,\n                 gamma=1.0,\n                 gm_scale=0.9,\n                 kl_scale=0.01,\n                 repetition_penalty=1.0,\n                 clean_up_tokenization_spaces=True,\n                 include_context_in_generation=False):\n        # Tokenize text\n        tokenized_cond_text = self.tokenizer.encode(self.tokenizer.bos_token + cond_text)\n\n        class_id = get_class_id(self.discrim, class_label)\n\n        pert_gen_tok_texts = []\n        for i in range(num_samples):\n            pert_gen_tok_text, discrim_loss, loss_in_time = generate_text_pplm(\n                model=self.model,\n                context=tokenized_cond_text,\n                device=self.device,\n                perturb=True,\n                classifier=self.classifier,\n                class_label=class_id,\n                length=length,\n                stepsize=stepsize,\n                temperature=temperature,\n                top_k=top_k,\n                sample=sample,\n Abstract of the paper RealToxicityPrompts: Evaluating Neural Toxic Degeneration in Language  Models:\nPretrained neural language models (LMs) are prone to generating racist,\nsexist, or otherwise toxic language which hinders their safe deployment. We\ninvestigate the extent to which pretrained LMs can be prompted to generate\ntoxic language, and the effectiveness of controllable text generation\nalgorithms at preventing such toxic degeneration. Using RealToxicityPrompts, we\nfind that pretrained LMs can degenerate into toxic text even from seemingly\ninnocuous prompts. We empirically assess several controllable generation\nmethods, and find that while data- or compute-intensive methods (e.g., adaptive\npretraining on non-toxic data) are more effective at steering away from\ntoxicity than simpler solutions (e.g., banning "bad" words), no current method\nis failsafe against neural toxic degeneration. \\"url\\": \\"https://s3.amazonaws.com/models.huggingface.co/bert/pplm/discriminators/SST_classifier_head.pt\\",\\n        \\"class_size\\": 5,\\n        \\"embed_size\\": 1024,\\n        \\"class_vocab\\": {\\"very_positive\\": 2, \\"very_negative\\": 3},\\n        \\"default_class\\": 3,\\n        \\"pretrained_model\\": \\"gpt2-medium\\",\\n    },\\n    \\"toxicity\\": {\\n        \\"url\\": \\"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uber-research/PPLM/master/paper_code/discrim_models/toxicity_classifierhead.pt\\",\\n        \\"class_size\\": 2,\\n        \\"embed_size\\": 1024,\\n        \\"class_vocab\\": {\\"non_toxic\\": 0, \\"toxic\\": 1},\\n        \\"default_class\\": 0,\\n        \\"pretrained_model\\": \\"gpt2-medium\\",\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n\\ndef to_var(x, requires_grad=False, volatile=False, device=\\"cuda\\"):\\n    if torch.cuda.is_available() and device == \\"cuda\\":\\n        x = x.cuda()\\n    elif device != \\"cuda\\":\\n        x = x.to(device)\\n    return Variable(x, requires_grad=requires_grad, volatile=volatile)\\n\\n\\ndef top_k_filter(logits, k, probs=False):\\n    \\"\\"\\"\\n    Masks everything but the k top entries as -infinity (1e10).\\n    Used to mask logits such that e^-infinity -> 0 won\'t contribute to the\\n    sum of the denominator.\\n    \\"\\"\\"\\n    if k == 0:\\n        return logits\\n    else:\\n        values = torch.topk(logits, k)[0]\\n        batch_mins = values[:, -1].view(-1, 1).expand_as(logits)\\n        if probs:\\n            return torch.where(logits < batch_mins, torch.ones_like(logits) * 0.0, logits)\\n        return torch.where(logits < batch_mins, torch.ones_like(logits) * -BIG_CONST, logits)\\n\\n\\ndef get_classifier(name: str, device: str) -> ClassificationHead:\\n    params = DISCRIMINATOR_MODELS_PARAMS[name]\\n    classifier = ClassificationHead(class_size=params[\\"class_size\\"], embed_size=params[\\"embed_size\\"]).to(device)\\n    if \\"url\\" in params:\\n        resolved_archive_file = cached_path(params[\\"url\\"])\\n    elif \\"path\\" in params:\\n        resolved_archive_file = params[\\"path\\"]\\n    else:\\n        raise ValueError(\\"Either url or path have to be specified \\" \\"in the discriminator model parameters\\")\\n    classifier.load_state_dict(torch.load(resolved_archive_file, map_location=device))\\n    classifier.eval()\\n    return classifier\\n\\n\\ndef get_class_id(name: str, class_label: Union[str, int]) -> Optional[int]:\\n    params = DISCRIMINATOR_MODELS_PARAMS[name]\\n    if isinstance(class_label, str):\\n        if class_label in params[\\"class_vocab\\"]:\\n            label_id = params[\\"class_vocab\\"][class_label]\\n        else:\\n            label_id = params[\\"default_class\\"]\\n            print(\\"class_label {} not in class_vocab\\".format(class_label))\\n            print(\\"available values are: {}\\".format(params[\\"class_vocab\\"]))\\n            print(\\"using default class {}\\".format(label_id))\\n    elif isinstance(class_label, int):\\n        if class_label in set(params[\\"class_vocab\\"].values()):\\n            label_id = class_label\\n        else:\\n            label_id = params[\\"default_class\\"]\\n            print(\\"class_label {} not in class_vocab\\".format(class_label))\\n            print(\\"available values are: {}\\".format(params[\\"class_vocab\\"]))\\n            print(\\"using default class {}\\".format(label_id))\\n    else:\\n        label_id = params[\\"default_class\\"]\\n    return label_id\\n\\n\\ndef perturb_past(\\n        past,\\n        model,\\n        last,\\n        unpert_past=None,\\n        unpert_logits=None,\\n        accumulated_hidden=None,\\n        grad_norms=None,\\n        stepsize=0.01,\\n        classifier=None,\\n        class_label=None,\\n        num_iterations=3,\\n        horizon_length=1,\\n        window_length=0,\\n        decay=False,\\n        gamma=1.5,\\n        kl_scale=0.01,\\n        device=\\"cuda\\",\\n):\\n    # Generate inital perturbed past\\n    grad_accumulator = [(np.zeros(p.shape).astype(\\"float32\\")) for p in past]\\n\\n    if accumulated_hidden is None:\\n        accumulated_hidden = 0\\n\\n    if decay:\\n        decay_mask = torch.arange(0.0, 1.0 + SMALL_CONST, 1.0 / (window_length))[1:]\\n    else:\\n        decay_mask = 1.0\\n\\n    # Generate a mask is gradient perturbated is based on a past window\\n    _, _, _, curr_length, _ = past[0].shape\\n\\n    if curr_length > window_length and window_length > 0:\\n        ones_key_val_shape = tuple(past[0].shape[:-2]) + tuple([window_length]) + tuple(past[0].shape[-1:])\\n\\n        zeros_key_val_shape = (\\n                tuple(past[0].shape[:-2]) + tuple([curr_length - window_length]) + tuple(past[0].shape[-1:])\\n        )\\n\\n        ones_mask = torch.ones(ones_key_val_shape)\\n        ones_mask = decay_mask * ones_mask.permute(0, 1, 2, 4, 3)\\n        ones_mask = ones_mask.permute(0, 1, 2, 4, 3)\\n\\n        window_mask = torch.cat((ones_mask, torch.zeros(zeros_key_val_shape)), dim=-2).to(device)\\n    else:\\n        window_mask = torch.ones_like(past[0]).to(device)\\n\\n    # accumulate perturbations for num_iterations\\n    loss_per_iter = []\\n    new_accumulated_hidden = None\\n    for i in range(num_iterations):\\n        curr_perturbation = [\\n            to_var(torch.from_numpy(p_), requires_grad=True, device=device) for p_ in grad_accumulator\\n        ]\\n\\n        # Compute hidden using perturbed past\\n        perturbed_past = list(map(add, past, curr_perturbation))\\n        _, _, _, curr_length, _ = curr_perturbation[0].shape\\n        all_logits, _, all_hidden = model(last, past=perturbed_past)\\n        hidden = all_hidden[-1]\\n        new_accumulated_hidden = accumulated_hidden + torch.sum(hidden, dim=1).detach()\\n        logits = all_logits[:, -1, :]\\n        probs = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1)\\n\\n        loss = 0.0\\n        loss_list = []\\n\\n        ce_loss = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()\\n        curr_unpert_past = unpert_past\\n        curr_probs = torch.unsqueeze(probs, dim=1)\\n        wte = model.resize_token_embeddings()\\n        for _ in range(horizon_length):\\n            inputs_embeds = torch.matmul(curr_probs, wte.weight.data)\\n            _, curr_unpert_past, curr_all_hidden = model(past=curr_unpert_past, inputs_embeds=inputs_embeds)\\n            curr_hidden = curr_all_hidden[-1]\\n            new_accumulated_hidden = new_accumulated_hidden + torch.sum(curr_hidden, dim=1)\\n\\n        prediction = classifier(new_accumulated_hidden / (curr_length + 1 + horizon_length))\\n\\n        label = torch.tensor(prediction.shape[0] * [class_label], device=device, dtype=torch.long)\\n        discrim_loss = ce_loss(prediction, label)\\n        loss += discrim_loss\\n        loss_list.append(discrim_loss)\\n\\n        if kl_scale > 0.0:\\n            unpert_probs = F.softmax(unpert_logits[:, -1, :], dim=-1)\\n            unpert_probs = unpert_probs + SMALL_CONST * (unpert_probs <= SMALL_CONST).float().to(device).detach()\\n            correction = SMALL_CONST * (probs <= SMALL_CONST).float().to(device).detach()\\n            corrected_probs = probs + correction.detach()\\n            kl_loss = kl_scale * ((corrected_probs * (corrected_probs / unpert_probs).log()).sum())\\n            loss += kl_loss\\n\\n        loss_per_iter.append(loss.data.cpu().numpy())\\n\\n        # compute gradients\\n        loss.backward()\\n\\n        # calculate gradient norms\\n        grad_norms = [\\n            (torch.norm(p_.grad * window_mask) + SMALL_CONST) for index, p_ in enumerate(curr_perturbation)\\n        ]\\n\\n        # normalize gradients\\n        grad = [\\n            -stepsize * (p_.grad * window_mask / grad_norms[index] ** gamma).data.cpu().numpy()\\n            for index, p_ in enumerate(curr_perturbation)\\n        ]\\n\\n        # accumulate gradient\\n        grad_accumulator = list(map(add, grad, grad_accumulator))\\n\\n        # reset gradients, just to make sure\\n        for p_ in curr_perturbation:\\n            p_.grad.data.zero_()\\n\\n        # removing past from the graph\\n        new_past = []\\n        for p_ in past:\\n            new_past.append(p_.detach())\\n        past = new_past\\n\\n    # apply the accumulated perturbations to the past\\n    grad_accumulator = [to_var(torch.from_numpy(p_), requires_grad=True, device=device) for p_ in grad_accumulator]\\n    pert_past = list(map(add, past, grad_accumulator))\\n\\n    return pert_past, new_accumulated_hidden, grad_norms, loss_per_iter\\n\\n\\ndef generate_text_pplm(\\n        model,\\n        context=None,\\n        past=None,\\n        device=\\"cuda\\",\\n        perturb=True,\\n        classifier=None,\\n        class_label=None,\\n        length=100,\\n        stepsize=0.02,\\n        temperature=1.0,\\n        top_k=10,\\n        sample=False,\\n        num_iterations=3,\\n        grad_length=10000,\\n        horizon_length=1,\\n        window_length=0,\\n        decay=False,\\n        gamma=1.5,\\n        gm_scale=0.9,\\n        kl_scale=0.01,\\n        repetition_penalty=1.0,\\n):\\n    output_so_far = None\\n    if context:\\n        context_t = torch.tensor(context, device=device, dtype=torch.long)\\n        while len(context_t.shape) < 2:\\n            context_t = context_t.unsqueeze(0)\\n        output_so_far = context_t\\n\\n    grad_norms = None\\n    last = None\\n    unpert_discrim_loss = 0\\n    loss_in_time = []\\n    for i in trange(length, desc=\'Generating with PPLM\', disable=True):\\n\\n        # Get past/probs for current output, except for last word\\n        # Note that GPT takes 2 inputs: past + current_token\\n\\n        # run model forward to obtain unperturbed\\n        if past is None and output_so_far is not None:\\n            last = output_so_far[:, -1:]\\n            if output_so_far.shape[1] > 1:\\n                _, past, _ = model(output_so_far[:, :-1])\\n\\n        unpert_logits, unpert_past, unpert_all_hidden = model(output_so_far)\\n        unpert_last_hidden = unpert_all_hidden[-1]\\n\\n        # check if we are abowe grad max length\\n        if i >= grad_length:\\n            current_stepsize = stepsize * 0\\n        else:\\n            current_stepsize = stepsize\\n\\n        # modify the past if necessary\\n        if not perturb or num_iterations == 0:\\n            pert_past = past\\n\\n        else:\\n            accumulated_hidden = unpert_last_hidden[:, :-1, :]\\n            accumulated_hidden = torch.sum(accumulated_hidden, dim=1)\\n\\n            if past is not None:\\n                pert_past, _, grad_norms, loss_this_iter = perturb_past(\\n                    past,\\n                    model,\\n                    last,\\n                    unpert_past=unpert_past,\\n                    unpert_logits=unpert_logits,\\n                    accumulated_hidden=accumulated_hidden,\\n                    grad_norms=grad_norms,\\n                    stepsize=current_stepsize,\\n                    classifier=classifier,\\n                    class_label=class_label,\\n                    num_iterations=num_iterations,\\n                    horizon_length=horizon_length,\\n                    window_length=window_length,\\n                    decay=decay,\\n                    gamma=gamma,\\n                    kl_scale=kl_scale,\\n                    device=device,\\n                )\\n                loss_in_time.append(loss_this_iter)\\n            else:\\n                pert_past = past\\n\\n        pert_logits, past, pert_all_hidden = model(last, past=pert_past)\\n        pert_logits = pert_logits[:, -1, :] / temperature  # + SMALL_CONST\\n\\n        for token_idx in set(output_so_far[0].tolist()):\\n            if pert_logits[0, token_idx] < 0:\\n                pert_logits[0, token_idx] *= repetition_penalty\\n            else:\\n                pert_logits[0, token_idx] /= repetition_penalty\\n\\n        pert_probs = F.softmax(pert_logits, dim=-1)\\n\\n        if classifier is not None:\\n            ce_loss = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()\\n            prediction = classifier(torch.mean(unpert_last_hidden, dim=1))\\n            label = torch.tensor([class_label], device=device, dtype=torch.long)\\n            unpert_discrim_loss = ce_loss(prediction, label)\\n            # print(\\"unperturbed discrim loss\\", unpert_discrim_loss.data.cpu().numpy())\\n        else:\\n            unpert_discrim_loss = 0\\n\\n        # Fuse the modified model and original model\\n        if perturb:\\n\\n            unpert_probs = F.softmax(unpert_logits[:, -1, :], dim=-1)\\n\\n            pert_probs = (pert_probs ** gm_scale) * (unpert_probs ** (1 - gm_scale))  # + SMALL_CONST\\n            pert_probs = top_k_filter(pert_probs, k=top_k, probs=True)  # + SMALL_CONST\\n\\n            # rescale\\n            if torch.sum(pert_probs) <= 1:\\n                pert_probs = pert_probs / torch.sum(pert_probs)\\n\\n        else:\\n            pert_logits = top_k_filter(pert_logits, k=top_k)  # + SMALL_CONST\\n            pert_probs = F.softmax(pert_logits, dim=-1)\\n\\n        # sample or greedy\\n        if sample:\\n            last = torch.multinomial(pert_probs, num_samples=1)\\n\\n        else:\\n            _, last = torch.topk(pert_probs, k=1, dim=-1)\\n\\n        # update context/output_so_far appending the new token\\n        output_so_far = last if output_so_far is None else torch.cat((output_so_far, last), dim=1)\\n\\n        # print(tokenizer.decode(output_so_far.tolist()[0]))\\n\\n    return output_so_far, unpert_discrim_loss, loss_in_time\\n\\n\\nclass PPLMGeneration(Pipeline):\\n    def __init__(self,\\n                 discrim: str,\\n                 seed=0,\\n                 **kwargs):\\n        # Set random seed\\n        torch.manual_seed(seed)\\n        np.random.seed(seed)\\n\\n        pretrained_model = DISCRIMINATOR_MODELS_PARAMS[discrim][\\"pretrained_model\\"]\\n        print(\\"discrim = {}, pretrained_model set \\" \\"to discriminator\'s = {}\\".format(discrim, pretrained_model))\\n\\n        # GPT2Tokenizer.from_pretrained(pretrained_model)\\n\\n        # Freeze GPT-2 weights\\n        for param in model.parameters():\\n            param.requires_grad = False\\n\\n        super().__init__(model=model, tokenizer=tokenizer, **kwargs)\\n\\n        # Additional setup after creating model and tokenizer\\n        self.discrim = discrim\\n        classifier = get_classifier(self.discrim, self.device)\\n        self.classifier = classifier\\n\\n    # Default parameters correspond to those in the PPLM paper for the toxicity discriminative model\\n    # Others (such as sampling) taken from https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/tree/master/examples/pplm\\n    def __call__(self,\\n                 cond_text=\'\',\\n                 num_samples=1,\\n                 class_label=-1,\\n                 length=20,\\n                 stepsize=0.02,\\n                 temperature=1.0,\\n                 top_k=10,\\n                 sample=True,\\n                 num_iterations=10,\\n                 grad_length=10000,\\n                 horizon_length=1,\\n                 window_length=0,\\n                 decay=False,\\n                 gamma=1.0,\\n                 gm_scale=0.9,\\n                 kl_scale=0.01,\\n                 repetition_penalty=1.0,\\n                 clean_up_tokenization_spaces=True,\\n              class_label=class_id,\\n                length=length,\\n                stepsize=stepsize,\\n                temperature=temperature,\\n                top_k=top_k,\\n                sample=sample,\\n                num_iterations=num_iterations,\\n                grad_length=grad_length,\\n                horizon_length=horizon_length,\\n                window_length=window_length,\\n                decay=decay,\\n                gamma=gamma,\\n                gm_scale=gm_scale,\\n                kl_scale=kl_scale,\\n                repetition_penalty=repetition_penalty,\\n            )\\n            pert_gen_tok_texts.append(pert_gen_tok_text)\\n\\n        decode_start_idx = 0 if include_context_in_generation else self.tokenizer.decode(pert_gen_tok_text, clean_up_tokenization_spaces=clean_up_tokenization_spaces)\\n            for pert_gen_tok_text in pert_gen_tok_texts\\n        ]\\n\\n        return
